skill.name: firebase-firestore
skill.description: Guides agents through Cloud Firestore setup, database selection, security rules, SDK usage, data modeling, indexes, and Firebase CLI workflows.
skill.summary: Plan and implement Cloud Firestore setup, rules, indexes, SDK usage, and database operations.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, and credential patterns. Manual review shows most are markdown examples for Firebase SDKs, Firestore rules, and security testing, but the skill does instruct agents to run Firebase CLI commands through npx and deploy or create Firestore resources. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious exfiltration was found; publish with a warning about external commands and cloud mutations.

medium_findings.title: External Firebase CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill tells agents to run Firebase CLI commands with npx and firebase-tools@latest. This is legitimate Firestore management, but it can download executable code, use credentials, and act on a Firebase project.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command strings are explicit and are part of the intended workflow. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the commands target Firebase administration.

medium_findings.title: Cloud Resource Mutation Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill includes commands that create Firestore databases and deploy rules or indexes. These operations can change live cloud resources if run against a real project.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly show create and deploy workflows. They are expected for the skill, but they require explicit user approval and project verification.

medium_findings.title: Local Firebase Configuration Writes
medium_findings.description: The provisioning guides direct agents to create or update firebase.json, firestore.rules, and firestore.indexes.json. Incorrect edits could weaken rules or disrupt deployment configuration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-write instructions are explicit. This is a normal setup capability, but mistakes can affect application security and deployment behavior.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Shell Command Substitution Matches
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged $() syntax in Firestore Rules path interpolation. The reviewed lines are fenced documentation examples, not host shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The syntax appears inside Firestore Rules examples that use /databases/$(database)/documents paths. This is not shell command substitution.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Credential Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged private-key wording in a defensive checklist. The text asks users to test for data exposure and does not include secrets or exfiltration logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is an attack checklist for hardening Firestore rules. No actual key, certificate, seed phrase, or outbound transfer is present.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Crypto Matches
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts map to Firestore query and index examples, not cryptographic algorithms. No reviewed file implements hashing, encryption, or key derivation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is about Firestore query operators, field ordering, and SDK calls. The static labels do not match the semantic context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unpinned Latest CLI Package
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends firebase-tools@latest through npx. Using latest can change behavior between runs and downloads executable tooling at execution time.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The @latest package reference is explicit. This is common CLI usage, but it increases supply-chain and reproducibility risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Firestore Deploy Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The guides include deploy commands for Firestore rules and indexes. These commands can alter cloud authorization behavior for a project.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The deploy commands are explicit and security-sensitive. They are legitimate only when the user has selected the intended project and reviewed generated rules.

dangerous_patterns.title: Firestore Database Creation Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can guide creation of new Firestore databases. This may create billable or persistent cloud resources in the selected Firebase project.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The create commands are concrete and target cloud resources. The behavior is expected for this skill but should be gated by user confirmation.

content.value_statement: Firestore setup can be complex across rules, indexes, SDKs, and database editions. This skill guides agents through practical setup, secure rules, and client integration steps.
actual_capabilities: Identifies Standard or Enterprise Firestore database editions before setup.
Guides Firebase CLI commands for listing, creating, and deploying Firestore resources.
Provides security rules generation guidance with validation and attack testing steps.
Explains SDK usage patterns for web, Android, iOS, Flutter, and Python references.
Documents index configuration patterns for Standard and Enterprise editions.
Supports local emulator workflows for Firestore development and testing.
limitations: Requires Firebase project access and credentials for CLI operations.
Does not guarantee generated security rules are production ready.
Uses Firebase CLI examples that may change with Firebase tooling releases.
Enterprise guidance depends on the installed SDK and available pipeline APIs.

use_cases.title: Provision a Firestore Project
use_cases.description: Set up database configuration, rules, indexes, and deployment commands for a new Firebase application.

use_cases.title: Harden Security Rules
use_cases.description: Generate prototype Firestore rules, validate access patterns, and test common authorization failures before release.

use_cases.title: Plan Query and Index Strategy
use_cases.description: Map application queries to the indexes and SDK patterns needed for reliable Firestore performance.

prompt_templates.title: Find My Firestore Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect my Firebase project files and tell me which Firestore edition and setup path I should use.

prompt_templates.title: Create Starter Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my data model and generate prototype Firestore security rules with clear assumptions and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Add SDK Queries
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me implement Firestore reads, writes, and real-time updates for my app using the matching SDK guide.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Rules and Indexes
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my Firestore rules, queries, and indexes for authorization gaps, missing validation, and deployment risks.

output_examples.input: A developer asks for help setting up Firestore in a new Firebase project.
output_examples.output: A setup plan that identifies the database edition, required project files, CLI commands to review, and deployment checks.

output_examples.input: A team needs secure rules for user-owned documents and public profile data.
output_examples.output: A rules design summary with owner checks, separate public and private data guidance, validation requirements, and test cases.

output_examples.input: An application query fails because Firestore needs an index.
output_examples.output: An index recommendation that explains the query shape, required fields, deployment path, and emulator validation step.

best_practices: Confirm the active Firebase project before running any CLI command.
Review generated security rules with real access tests before deployment.
Use the emulator for local validation before changing cloud resources.
anti_patterns: Running deploy commands without checking the selected project first.
Treating prototype security rules as production guarantees.
Allowing broad authenticated reads on collections that contain private user data.

faq.question: Does this skill run Firebase commands automatically?
faq.answer: It provides command guidance. Agents should ask for confirmation before commands that affect cloud resources.

faq.question: Can it create a Firestore database?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Firebase CLI workflows for database creation when the user selects a location and project.

faq.question: Does it support Firestore Enterprise?
faq.answer: Yes. It has separate Enterprise references for provisioning, data modeling, SDK usage, security rules, and indexes.

faq.question: Are generated security rules final?
faq.answer: No. The skill frames rules as prototypes that need review, testing, and hardening before broad release.

faq.question: Which client SDKs are covered?
faq.answer: The references cover web, Android, iOS, Flutter, and Python usage depending on the database edition.

faq.question: What should users check before deployment?
faq.answer: Users should confirm the Firebase project, review rule changes, run emulator tests, and validate required indexes.
